outlook.headline: HBM packaging demand stays hot across the supply chain
outlook.summary: The next 1-2 quarters still point to improving HBM packaging demand, with Applied Materials, KLA, Kulicke & Soffa, and Entegris all citing hot/tight conditions tied to AI-driven buildouts. The strength is broadening across tools, materials, and hybrid-bonding/die-stacking workflows, while GlobalFoundries adds a capacity-expansion read; Onto Innovation offers no fresh confirmation. Watch whether the flat mention trend starts to climb, or whether this remains a strong but already-mature cycle.

drivers.label: AI-driven HBM packaging buildout remains hot
drivers.detail: Multiple suppliers describe HBM packaging as a major focus with tight supply and strong demand, indicating continued near-term pull-through.

drivers.label: Advanced packaging tool demand broadens
drivers.detail: HBM-related packaging is supporting more demand for advanced packaging tools and materials, lifting the whole ecosystem.

drivers.label: Hybrid bonding and die-stacking adoption
drivers.detail: Kulicke & Soffa highlights hybrid bonding and die-stacking alternatives as emerging HBM packaging demand drivers, suggesting ongoing technology migration.

drivers.label: Capacity expansion in growth technologies
drivers.detail: GlobalFoundries ties the advanced packaging context to growth technologies and capacity expansion, which should help backend ecosystem activity.

risks: Mention trend is flat at 0.1x with no momentum, so enthusiasm is strong but not accelerating.
Onto Innovation is neutral and provides no direct confirmation, limiting breadth of the read-through.
Any pause in AI/HBM buildouts or packaging capacity additions could ease the current tightness quickly.
